product_overview: A small session-authenticated PHP backend and companion mobile client to manage student records (list, create, update, delete) with UX behaviors like optimistic updates, retry, and accessibility support.
core_goals: Expose reliable CRUD APIs for student records with clear success and error semantics.
Ensure client UX handles optimistic updates, rollbacks, network errors, and empty/skeleton/retry states.
Enforce input validation and consistent authentication for mutation endpoints to prevent unauthorized changes.
Verify accessibility and consistent UI behaviors across add/edit/delete/search/sort flows.

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Server-side session login, logout, and session introspection so the client can establish and validate authenticated sessions.
user_flows: POST /api/login.php with valid username and password -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok', message: '...'} and session cookie -> GET /api/me.php with session cookie -> Receive 200 {authenticated: true, user: '<username>'}
POST /api/login.php with invalid password -> Receive 200 {status: 'failed', message: '<error>'} -> GET /api/me.php without session cookie -> Receive 200 {authenticated: false, user: null}

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok'} and session cookie -> POST /api/logout.php with session cookie -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok'} -> GET /api/me.php -> Receive 200 {authenticated: false, user: null}

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok'} and session cookie -> (idle until session expires) -> POST /api/create_student.php with expired session cookie -> Receive 401 Unauthorized (or 200 {status:'failed', message:'auth required'})

features.name: Student Management API
features.description: CRUD endpoints for student records: list all students (open), and create/update/delete mutations (create is protected by auth guard; update/delete enforcement is currently inconsistent and requires validation).
user_flows: GET /api/students.php -> Receive 200 Student[] (each item includes id, firstname, lastname, ratings)

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok'} and session cookie -> POST /api/create_student.php with session cookie and body {firstname, lastname, ratings} -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok', message: '<created>'} -> GET /api/students.php with session cookie -> Receive 200 Student[] including the new record
POST /api/create_student.php without session cookie -> Receive 401 Unauthorized (auth required) or 200 {status: 'failed', message: 'auth required'}

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> POST /api/update_student.php with session cookie and body {id, firstname, lastname, ratings} -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok', message: '<updated>'} -> GET /api/students.php -> Receive 200 Student[] showing updated values
POST /api/update_student.php with body containing non-existent id -> Receive 200 {status: 'failed', message: 'not found'}

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> POST /api/delete_student.php with session cookie and body {id} -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok', message: '<deleted>'} -> GET /api/students.php -> Receive 200 Student[] without the deleted record
POST /api/delete_student.php with body containing non-existent id -> Receive 200 {status: 'failed', message: 'not found'}

POST /api/update_student.php without session cookie -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok' | 'failed', message: '...'} (known limitation: mutation auth enforcement may be inconsistent; this flow demonstrates the risk if enforcement is missing)

features.name: Optimistic UI & Error Handling (Client UX)
features.description: Client-side behaviors for optimistic create/update/delete, rollback on failure, skeleton loading, retry flows, and empty-state handling to keep UI responsive and predictable.
user_flows: POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> POST /api/create_student.php with session cookie and valid body -> Receive 200 {status: 'ok'} (happy-path: client shows row immediately and retains it after server success)

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> Client shows temporary row and POST /api/create_student.php with session cookie and valid body -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error -> GET /api/students.php with session cookie -> Receive 200 Student[] not including the temporary record (client should rollback and show error)

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> Client applies optimistic update and POST /api/update_student.php with session cookie and valid body -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error -> GET /api/students.php -> Receive 200 Student[] with original values restored (client rollback)

POST /api/login.php with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> Client removes row optimistically and POST /api/delete_student.php with session cookie and body {id} -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error -> GET /api/students.php -> Receive 200 Student[] with deleted row present (client must re-insert and notify user)

GET /api/students.php (on slow network) -> Receive delayed 200 Student[] or network error -> Client shows skeleton loading, then either list or error+retry button -> On retry: GET /api/students.php -> Receive 200 Student[]

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Session login/logout and auth session introspection endpoints.

endpoints.description: Authenticate user and create server-side session.
Destroy active session for authenticated user.

features.name: Student Management API
features.description: CRUD operations for student records with create endpoint protected by auth guard.

known_limitations.issue: Inconsistent authentication enforcement across mutation endpoints.
known_limitations.impact: Update/delete actions may be callable without authentication.

known_limitations.issue: Input validation rules are minimal and may allow malformed values.
known_limitations.impact: Invalid data may be accepted or produce inconsistent error handling.
